title: rosa canina var. calophylla
intro: Rosa canina var. calophylla, also known as the scotch rose or wild rose, is a subspecies of the wild rose plant (Rosa canina), a deciduous shrub native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. The medicinal properties of Rosa canina var. calophylla include its high content of vitamins A and C, as well as antioxidants and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to improve cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and support immune function, as well as its use in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues and skin conditions. Rosa canina var. calophylla can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be made by steeping the dried flowers, leaves, and stems in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
benefits: Rosa canina var. calophylla has many benefits, such as its ability to promote healthy skin and reduce inflammation due to its high levels of vitamin C and antioxidants, which can help to alleviate conditions like acne, rosacea, and eczema. This plant also has antimicrobial properties, making it effective against infections and wound healing, and has been used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and ulcers, by reducing inflammation and promoting the growth of beneficial gut bacteria. Additionally, the extracts of Rosa canina var. calophylla have been shown to have anti-cancer properties and may help to reduce the risk of certain types of cancer, such as breast and prostate cancer, by inhibiting the growth of cancer cells and promoting apoptosis. By incorporating this plant into our lives, we can improve our overall health, increase our energy levels, and enhance our well-being, ultimately leading to a more vibrant and fulfilling life.
actions: Rosa canina var. calophylla has many therapeutic actions, such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of skin irritations, wounds, and infections, ultimately promoting faster healing and reduced discomfort for individuals. The plant's antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects can also aid in soothing and calming irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling, and promoting a sense of relaxation and well-being. Furthermore, the therapeutic properties of Rosa canina var. calophylla may help to regulate blood sugar levels, reduce cholesterol and triglyceride levels, and improve cardiovascular health, all of which can contribute to a reduced risk of chronic diseases and improved overall quality of life. By harnessing the therapeutic actions of this plant, individuals can enjoy improved physical and mental well-being, increased energy levels, and enhanced resilience to stress and illness.
intro_study: According to "African journal of traditional, complementary, and alternative medicines : AJTCAM", rosa canina var. calophylla benefits from its antioxidant and α-amylase inhibition activities which can help in the management of diabetes mellitus.
constituents: Rosa canina var. calophylla has many active constituents, such as flavonoids, including quercetin, kaempferol, and isorhapontigenin, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, reducing joint pain and swelling in individuals. The plant also contains phenolic acids, like salicylic acid, which acts as an analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent, providing relief from headaches, fever, and other inflammatory conditions. Additionally, Rosa canina var. calophylla is rich in ascorbic acid, also known as vitamin C, which supports immune function, boosts collagen production, and aids in wound healing, making it beneficial for people with compromised immune systems or skin issues. The plant's high content of carotenoids, such as beta-carotene, also contributes to its medicinal value by protecting against oxidative stress, promoting healthy vision, and supporting skin health, ultimately improving overall well-being.
